cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: How AI discovery is changing everything marketers measure
stories.plain: AI-powered search and answer engines are sending far less traffic to brand websites, making traditional metrics like page views and click-through rates misleading. Marketers need new signals — share of AI-generated answers, brand mentions in model outputs, and downstream conversion — to understand whether their content is actually reaching buyers. GEO is now a measurement problem, not just a content problem.

stories.head: Stensul announces major Governed Creation release for AI-era marketing stack
stories.plain: Stensul updated its platform to govern AI-generated campaign content as it is created, adding integrations with Figma, WRITER, and Salesforce Marketing Cloud Next. Enterprise teams can now route AI-produced copy and design into approval workflows before launch. This matters because governing AI output at the creation stage — not after — is the emerging standard for compliant, on-brand marketing at scale.

stories.head: 3 AI agents to improve marketing workflows
stories.plain: A practical guide to deploying agentic AI in marketing: use a shared data source, set measurable KPIs, and keep humans in the loop for strategy and quality checks. The piece cuts through vendor hype with concrete implementation advice. Useful for marketers deciding how to govern agentic systems before rolling them out across channels.

stories.head: Zappi launches Amplify AI for predictive ad testing at high volume
stories.plain: Zappi's new Amplify AI scores social video ads for likely performance before they go live, addressing the reality that most digital creatives ship untested. A connected Amplify Hub centralises ad insights. Predictive creative testing at scale changes how media budgets are allocated — moving quality control earlier in the workflow rather than relying on live A/B results.

stories.head: Telgorithm launches AI-powered RCS Business Messaging Suite with conversational billing
stories.plain: Telgorithm is among the first providers to offer session-based billing for conversational RCS messages, with AI-driven SMS fallback and Smart Queueing for reliability. RCS changes how brands message consumers — richer, app-like interactions inside the native texting thread — and session billing makes two-way conversations cost-predictable, reshaping how CEP vendors price real-time engagement.

stories.head: How AI discovery is changing everything marketers measure
stories.plain: AI-powered search tools like ChatGPT and Perplexity are now answering questions directly, so the traffic those queries used to send to websites is drying up. This article argues that traffic-based metrics are losing meaning and pushes marketers to adopt new measures of brand presence inside AI answers — a fundamental rethink of how marketing performance is evaluated.

stories.head: AI visibility depends on who writes about your brand
stories.plain: Being cited by ChatGPT or Perplexity isn't about SEO rank — it's about which third-party sources AI models trust when they form answers. This piece explains the editorial and authority signals that drive AI citation, giving marketers a practical framework for measuring and improving their brand's presence inside generative AI answers.

stories.head: HubSpot May 2026 updates: Breeze AI becomes an active work participant, not just a prompt tool
stories.plain: HubSpot's May release shifts Breeze from a prompted assistant to an agentic layer embedded in everyday workflows — handling data cleanup, automation, and content tasks without waiting to be asked. For teams evaluating CEPs, this signals how major all-in-one platforms are folding agentic AI directly into the product rather than bolting it on.

stories.head: 3 AI agents to improve marketing workflows
stories.plain: A practical guide to piloting AI agents inside real marketing teams, covering how to set a shared data source, choose KPIs that actually measure agent value, and keep humans in the loop for strategy and quality control. Useful for any marketer trying to move from chatbot experiments to governed, production-ready agentic systems.

stories.head: Stensul releases governed AI campaign creation with Figma, Salesforce MC Next, and personalization integrations
stories.plain: Stensul's new release adds governance checkpoints directly into the AI content creation flow — connecting Figma design, Writer copy, and Salesforce Marketing Cloud Next so that AI-generated campaigns pass brand and compliance review before they go live. This matters because it shows how enterprise martech is solving the approval bottleneck that stalls AI-produced content.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: AI Discovery Rewrites What Gets Measured
body: When users find products through ChatGPT, Claude, or Perplexity instead of a search engine, the click-through rate, keyword rank, and page-view metrics that underpin most marketing dashboards simply stop reflecting reality. AI-mediated discovery creates an invisible layer between content creation and customer arrival that traditional analytics cannot see.

Two signals are emerging as replacements: brand citation frequency inside AI-generated answers, and the editorial credibility of the third-party sources those models trust. Platforms like Scrunch are being built specifically to track this & surface it for brand teams, making "AI visibility" a distinct KPI category alongside SEO.
ev: Agile Brand · Scrunch AI visibility platform

themes.head: The Governed AI Stack Becomes Non-Negotiable
body: Generative AI in the marketing stack has moved fast enough that brand safety, compliance, and content guardrails are now the bottleneck, not the AI capability itself. Stensul's "Governed Creation" release and Heimdal's enterprise AI risk management report both frame control frameworks as the differentiating layer on top of commodity model access.

This shifts the vendor conversation: buyers are no longer asking whether a tool uses AI, but whether it lets them define, enforce, & audit the rules under which AI operates. Martech stacks without governance rails are increasingly viewed as liabilities rather than assets.
ev: Agile Brand · Heimdal AI risk management

themes.head: AI Agents Enter Everyday Workflows
body: Autonomous AI agents are shifting from proof-of-concept to operational tooling, with multiple platforms releasing agent-style features this month. HubSpot's Breeze enhancements, Workato's orchestration ratings, & step-by-step agent build guides all point to agents becoming a standard layer in the marketing operations toolkit rather than an experimental add-on.

The practical decision now is not whether to use agents but how to structure the handoff between human judgment and automated action. Teams that define those boundaries clearly gain speed; those that skip the design work are accumulating the hidden complexity costs the stack-fragmentation research describes.

themes.head: MCP Servers Unlock Tool-to-AI Connectivity
body: The Model Context Protocol is quietly becoming infrastructure: Wistia launched an MCP server so AI assistants can directly query video libraries, and Kythera Labs shipped a Clinical Semantic Bridge MCP on the Databricks Marketplace. MCP is the plumbing that lets purpose-built data assets talk natively to general-purpose AI models without custom integration work for every pair.

For martech buyers this matters because it changes the integration calculus. Instead of waiting for a vendor's native AI feature, teams can expose their existing data stores to any MCP-compatible model & build workflows immediately. Expect MCP compatibility to appear on RFP checklists within the next quarter.
ev: MarTech Series · Kythera MCP on Databricks

themes.head: Predictive Testing Replaces Post-Campaign Regret
body: Zappi's Amplify AI launch brings predictive performance scoring to high-volume digital campaigns before spend is committed, while Treasure AI's IDC MarketScape leadership recognition highlights that AI-enabled CDPs are now expected to surface predictions, not just segment audiences. The direction is clear: the testing phase is moving upstream of the media buy.

This compresses the traditional creative-test-learn cycle into something closer to real-time, which changes both agency workflows & in-house team structures. Teams still relying on post-campaign analysis as their primary optimization loop are a structural cycle behind competitors who test creatively before they publish.
ev: Agile Brand · Treasure AI IDC MarketScape
Agile Brand · revenue-driven AI marketing engine

month.takeaway: Across every layer of the stack this month, the unifying signal is that AI is migrating from a feature bolted onto existing tools into the connective tissue that determines which brands get found, trusted, and bought — making infrastructure choices (governance, MCP connectivity, predictive testing) as strategically consequential as creative decisions.
